text: Coffee originated in the ancient forests of the Ethiopian plateau, where wild Coffea arabica plants were first discovered. The invigorating beverage was later developed and commercialized on the Arabian Peninsula, becoming a global cultural and social phenomenon. About Coffee - National Coffee Association +2The journey of the world's most popular caffeinated beverage spans over a millennium, from ancient legends to modern café cultures. 1. The Legendary Origins (9th Century) Ethiopia: Legend holds that a 9th-century goat herder named Kaldi first discovered the energizing effects of coffee after noticing his goats became highly energetic after eating the cherries from a certain tree.Early Consumption: Before it was brewed as a hot drink, East African tribes would grind coffee cherries and mix them with animal fat to provide warriors with an energy boost. About Coffee - National Coffee Association +12. The Arabian Influence and the First Coffee Houses (15th–16th Century) Yemen: The beverage as we know it was likely born in Yemen, where Sufi Muslims brewed the roasted beans to stay awake during their nighttime spiritual devotions.Spread: Coffee quickly spread across the Middle East. The very first public coffee houses opened in Mecca and Constantinople (modern-day Istanbul) in the 16th century. Wikipedia +23. Arrival in Europe (17th Century) Venice: Venetian merchants introduced coffee to Europe around 1615. Initially met with religious suspicion, Pope Clement VIII famously "baptized" the drink, making it a legitimate Christian beverage.Café Culture: The first European café opened in Venice in 1683. Establishments like the legendary Caffè Florian in Piazza San Marco (opened in 1720) became famous hubs for artists, writers, and intellectuals. Lavazza US +14. Global Expansion The Americas: Coffee was brought to the Americas in the 17th century. By the 18th century, Latin American countries became massive producers, with Brazil eventually growing into the world's largest coffee producer.Italian Innovations: Italy fundamentally changed how coffee is made. Milanese engineer Luigi Bezzera invented the first espresso machine in 1901.Modern Specialty Era: The 1960s and 1970s sparked a specialty coffee movement, with the founding of chains like Starbucks in Seattle in 1971, evolving into today's focus on fair-trade, artisanal, and single-origin roasts. www.portioli.it +2
